items.title: Lectric Memorial Day Sale offers up to $694 in FREE gear + up to $500 price cuts on e-bikes starting from $799
items.summary:
Lectric has just launched its Memorial Day e-bike Sale, one of the brand’s biggest sale events of the year, not to mention of summer, and you’re able to score up to $720 total savings: up to $694 in FREE bundled gear alongside two models getting up to $500 price drops. Many of the brand’s popular e-bikes are getting increased bundle packages during this event, like my personal pick for all-around commuting versatility, the XP4 750 Folding Utility e-bike that comes with $473 in FREE gear at $1,299 shipped, while the standard XP4 e-bike is getting a FREE $227 bundle at $999 shipped. Without the savings on the bundles, these packages would normally run you $1,772 and $1,226, respectively. The standard XP4 bundles contain the usual LevelUp rack, suspension seat post, and accordion bike lock, while the XP4 750 e-bike gets those alongside an Elite headlight, a phone mount, a pair of mirrors, and a water-resistant pannier bag. Head below to learn more (or check out our hands-on review here) and browse the full lineup of Memorial Day deals.

more…

Already down from $2,799 and $3,499 price tags, you’ll currently find both options as low as $1,619 (at Amazon for the standalone station) and $2,399 (direct from Jackery for bundle) elsewhere, with these prices only beaten by the former exclusive deals we secured for them back during New Years. Today’s deals gives you $1,350 and $1,400 markdowns, landing them at the second-lowest prices we have tracked.

items.title: Bluetti’s latest 3,014Wh Elite 300 power station covers storms, outdoor adventures, more at exclusive low prices from $1,011
items.summary:
As part of Bluetti’s ongoing Hurricane Prep Sale with tons of exclusive low prices on power stations, we wanted to shine a spotlight on the brand’s newest Elite 300 Portable Power Station and its bundles that start from $1,011.08 shipped, after using our code 9TO5TOYS8OFF at checkout, beating out its Amazon pricing by $88. This new model hit the market back in March at a $2,299 full price, which recently got a chop down to a $1,199 MSRP to better align with market values, though it has been seen climbing up as high as $1,449 at Amazon. Previously, we’ve been seeing this station discounted to $1,199 since the initial launch deal that landed it at $1,099 (which dropped to today’s price with our code). That all-time low is coming back around during this sale, with our exclusive code giving you combined total savings of $188 off the new going rate ($438 off Amazon’s highest recent pricing) for the best price we have tracked. Head below to learn all about it and browse the bundle offers – plus, you can check out the full lineup of Hurricane Prep Sale offers on other models here.

items.title: Prep for summer swims with up to 40% Beatbot robot pool cleaner savings to return and new low prices starting from $499
items.summary:
Beatbot is currently running an Anniversary Sale that is not only giving folks an ample opportunity to save up to 40% on a wide array of robot pool cleaners, but also giving us some new low prices in the process. One such low is hitting the newest Sora 70 Robotic Pool Cleaner for $1,149 shipped, matching the price from its direct website. It was only released back in mid-February, with the discounts we’ve seen up until this month only having dropped costs from its $1,499 full price to $1,349 and $1,199. Now, during this event, you’re getting an even bigger $350 markdown that lands it at a new all-time low price – just in time to get your own ahead of prime summer weather! You can also find an in-depth rundown on how mine is performing in our latest hands-on review. Head below to check out all the other robot deals that start from $499, or you can also find them in the official Amazon storefront here.

The savings are still rolling in, with up to 50% off popular brands like Blue Buffalo, Zesty Paws, Furbo, and plenty more across nearly every pet category imaginable.

Whether you’re stocking up on food and treats, grabbing new toys, upgrading grooming essentials, or browsing health products, accessories, apparel, and everyday pet care gear, there are tons of deals worth checking out for your four-legged companions.

items.title: Kevin Hartz’s A* just closed its third fund with $450M
items.summary: The firm takes a generalist approach, backing companies across categories such as AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security. The average check size for this fund will be between $3 million and $5 million, with the aim to back at least 30 startups.

items.title: Former Tesla exec and Heron Power CEO Drew Baglino has founded a heat pump startup
items.summary: Sadi Thermal Machines is Drew Baglino's second startup since leaving Tesla in 2024.

items.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
items.summary: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his experience running the prominent startup accelerator Y Combinator, knew "founders who had control usually did not give it up."

items.title: Dali ship operator charged over deadly  Baltimore bridge collapse

items.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit

items.title: Android adds a feature to stop you from doomscrolling
items.summary: Google’s new Android feature, Pause Point, forces you to wait before opening distracting apps in an effort to curb addictive scrolling habits.

items.title: Google unveils Googlebook, a new line of AI-native laptops
items.summary: The company says Googlebooks, which are launching this fall, are the first laptops designed from the ground up for Gemini Intelligence to offer personal and proactive help.

items.title: Google’s ‘Create My Widget’ feature will let you vibe-code your own widgets
items.summary: To create a widget, users will be able to describe what they want using natural language. For example, you could ask the feature to "suggest three high-protein meal prep recipes every week" in order to get a custom dashboard that you can add and resize on your home screen.

items.title: Google brings agentic AI and vibe-coded widgets to Android
items.summary: Gemini Intelligence will also include Gboard-based dictation and form-filling capabilities.

items.title: Google launches new Android security feature to help uncover spyware attacks
